explanation: This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER mode (pullup) and both the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, then it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY) within N_DURATION time units. It uses template 14 to express this eventuality.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, then eventually within the next N_DURATION time steps, the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state.  The formula uses the implies operator to capture the conditional nature of the requirement. It also accounts for the fact that transition to STANDBY mode is not immediate.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (pullup) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, then eventually within N_DURATION time units, the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY).  It uses template 14 to express this behavior and handles the case where the conditions could be false.

This LTL property checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (good), then eventually within N_DURATION time steps the autopilot will transition to STANDBY state (bool_exp3). It uses template 14 which implements an implication.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2 is true) and the pilot is in control (standby is true) and sensor data is good (good is true), then eventually within N_DURATION time units the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state (bool_exp3 is true). This captures the requirement that the autopilot should change states from MANEUVER to STANDBY under the specified conditions.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good. The formula uses the implication operator (->) to express the condition, and the F operator to indicate the eventual transition to STANDBY within the specified time frame.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2 is true) and the pilot is in control (standby is true) and sensor data is good (good is true), then it will eventually (F) within N_DURATION time units transition to the STANDBY state (bool_exp3 is true). The chosen template is suitable because it describes that the transition happens after MANEUVER state.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units, given that the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good.  The formula uses the implication to state the condition. If the bool_exp2 is true (autopilot is in MANEUVER), then bool_exp3 must become true within N_DURATION time units.  Otherwise, if the condition is false, then the implication holds.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within 2 time units if the pilot is in control (standby) and the sensor data is good.  The use of G[0,2] allows for a short, defined time window of transition instead of an unbounded transition time. The implication operator -> ensures the transition to STANDBY only happens under the specified conditions.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2), it will eventually transition to STANDBY state (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good.  The property uses the G operator to ensure that this implication always holds and the F operator to specify that the transition to STANDBY occurs eventually in a bounded time frame.

This LTL specification states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2 = true), then it must eventually (F) transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units. The condition for the transition is that the pilot is in control (standby = true) and sensor data is good (good = true). This is captured by bool_exp3, which is true when both standby and good are true. The overall property is globally (G) enforced, meaning it must hold at all times.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2: pullup) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (good), then it must eventually transition to the STANDBY state (bool_exp3: state_is_STANDBY) within N_DURATION time units. This captures the requirement that the autopilot changes states from MANEUVER to STANDBY under the specified conditions.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2 = pullup) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (good), then eventually within N_DURATION time units, the autopilot will transition to STANDBY state (bool_exp3 = state_is_STANDBY). It uses template 14 which is suitable for expressing the transition behavior based on certain conditions. The implication G(bool_exp2 -> F[0, N_DURATION] bool_exp3) ensures that the transition happens only when condition is satisfied.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER mode and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to STANDBY mode within N_DURATION time units.  The formula uses the implication (->) to express the conditional relationship, and the Globally (G) operator to indicate that this condition must hold at all times. The Finally (F) operator specifies that the transition to STANDBY mode must occur within the specified time window. Note that this assumes the autopilot does not transition through other modes first.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (pullup) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, then eventually within N_DURATION time steps, the autopilot will transition to STANDBY state.  The formula uses implies operator to link the condition to the consequence and globally quantifies this implication for all time. The G operator ensures that the property is checked in all states. The F operator indicates that eventually the standby state will be reached. The use of F[0,N_DURATION] ensures it happens within the specified time duration.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2: state_is_MANEUVER) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (bool_exp3: standby & good), then eventually within N_DURATION time steps, the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state (bool_exp3: state_is_STANDBY). The chosen template ensures that the condition is checked globally (G) and that the transition to STANDBY occurs in the future (F).

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (pullup is true), it will eventually transition to STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY is true) within N_DURATION time units, given that the pilot is in control (standby is true) and sensor data is good (good is true). The implication (->) ensures the transition happens under the specified conditions. The Globally (G) operator ensures that this condition holds for all time steps.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good.  The property uses the implication operator to define the condition.  If the pilot is in control and sensor data is good, then the transition must occur within the specified time limit. Otherwise the autopilot can remain in MANEUVER state.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units, given that the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good.  The implication is used to capture the conditional nature of the transition, where the transition from MANEUVER to STANDBY is contingent on the pilot being in standby and sensor data being good. The G operator ensures this property holds globally, meaning that at every point in time, this conditional transition should occur.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (bool_exp3), then it will eventually transition to the STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units.  The use of G ensures that this implication holds globally across all time steps. The chosen template includes the possibility that the transition to STANDBY may also happen when the autopilot is not in MANEUVER state, making this translation more robust to unexpected system behaviors.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that when the autopilot is in MANEUVER state and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state.  The G operator ensures that this property holds globally, at all times. The -> operator represents implication, stating that if the condition on the left is true, then the condition on the right must also be true.  The F operator means that eventually the condition will hold.  The bool_exp2 represents the condition where the autopilot is in MANEUVER state, and the pilot is in control, and sensor data is good.  The bool_exp3 represents the condition where the autopilot is in STANDBY state. The N_DURATION is set to 1, indicating that the transition should happen within 1 time step.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (pullup) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, then it eventually transitions to STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units. The chosen template ensures that the condition holds globally (always) before the transition to STANDBY happens.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (bool_exp3), then it will eventually transition to STANDBY within N_DURATION time units. If the transition doesn't happen within the time window, then the property requires the autopilot to be in STANDBY state permanently.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER mode (pullup) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, then the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY).  The G operator ensures this holds globally across all time steps. The -> operator implies the transition. The F operator indicates that the property eventually becomes true. N_DURATION is set to 1 to capture the eventual transition, which could take one time step.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER mode (bool_exp2: state_is_MANEUVER) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (bool_exp3: standby & good), then eventually within N_DURATION time units the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). It does not specify when the autopilot transitions but rather that it should do so within a reasonable time.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2: pullup) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (good), then eventually within N_DURATION time units the autopilot transitions to STANDBY state (bool_exp3: state_is_STANDBY).  It uses template 14 to capture the implication that when the conditions are met, the autopilot will eventually reach the standby state within the given time frame.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2), then it must eventually transition to STANDBY state within N_DURATION time units (bool_exp3).  The boolean expression bool_exp2 is true when the autopilot is in MANEUVER mode and bool_exp3 is true when both the pilot is in control (standby) and the sensor data is good. The condition (bool_exp2 -> (F[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3)) ensures that if the autopilot is in MANEUVER mode, it will eventually transition to the STANDBY mode under the specified conditions. The outer 'G' operator ensures that this implication holds globally, meaning at every state.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (pullup) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, then it must eventually transition to STANDBY state. The N_DURATION parameter is set to 1, indicating that the transition should happen within the next time step.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2: pullup), it eventually transitions to STANDBY state (bool_exp3: state_is_STANDBY) within N_DURATION time units, given that the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (good). The implication (->) ensures that the transition happens only under the specified conditions. The Globally (G) operator ensures that this property holds for all timesteps.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2), it eventually transitions to STANDBY state (bool_exp3) within N_DURATION time units if the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good.  The formula uses implication (->) to express the conditional behavior of the autopilot. The G operator ensures that this behavior holds globally, and F operator is used to capture the "eventually" requirement.  The [0, N_DURATION] interval bounds the time it takes for the transition to occur.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2 = pullup) and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (good), then the autopilot will eventually transition to the STANDBY state (bool_exp3 = state_is_STANDBY) within N_DURATION time units. The use of G ensures that this property holds globally across all time steps. The implication (->) connects the condition (autopilot in MANEUVER and pilot in standby and sensor data good) to the consequence (eventually transitioning to STANDBY within a certain duration).  The F operator indicates that the STANDBY state will eventually be reached, but the [0, N_DURATION] bound ensures it occurs promptly.

This LTL specification asserts that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state, it will eventually transition to STANDBY state within 2 time units if the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good. The chosen template uses a combination of globally (G) and eventually (F) operators to capture the desired behavior.  bool_exp2 represents the condition for the transition (pilot in standby and sensor data good), and bool_exp3 represents the target state (STANDBY). N_DURATION is set to 2, indicating the maximum time allowed for the transition.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, then in the next state, the autopilot will be in STANDBY state. This captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from MANEUVER to STANDBY when the pilot takes over and the sensor data is good.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER mode (bool_exp2 = state_is_MANEUVER is true), and the pilot is in control (standby = true) and sensor data is good (good = true), then eventually within N_DURATION time units, the autopilot will transition to the STANDBY state (bool_exp3 = state_is_STANDBY becomes true).

The bool_exp2 represents the condition that the autopilot is in MANEUVER mode and sensor data is good, while bool_exp3 represents the condition that the autopilot transitions to the STANDBY state.

The formula uses the G operator to globally imply that this behavior must hold for all time steps. The F operator indicates that the event will eventually happen. This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2 = state_is_MANEUVER), it eventually transitions to STANDBY state (bool_exp3 = state_is_STANDBY) within N_DURATION time units, given that the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good (bool_exp1 = standby & good). The formula uses template 14 to capture this behavior. If the pilot is not in control or sensor data is not good, the implication is vacuously true, and the formula doesn't impose any requirements on the autopilot's behavior.

This LTL property states that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state and the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, then in the next state the autopilot will be in STANDBY state.  It uses the implication operator (->) to capture the "when" condition, and the next operator (X) to express the change in state in the next time step.

This LTL formula checks that whenever the autopilot is in MANEUVER state (bool_exp2 is true because pullup is true), it eventually transitions to STANDBY state (bool_exp3 is true because state_is_STANDBY is true) within N_DURATION time units, given that the pilot is in control (standby is true) and sensor data is good (good is true).  The G operator ensures this property holds globally (in every state). The -> operator implies that if the precondition (bool_exp2) is true, then the consequent (bool_exp3) must eventually hold.
